name: Peppy Dill Wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut large cucumbers lengthwise into quarters, then cut each quarter into 1 inch long pieces.
2. Combine cucumber, garlic (if using), and salt in a glass bowl. Cover with ice cubes. Let stand in a cool place for at least 6 hours or overnight.
3. Drain well.
4. In a saucepan, combine vinegar, water, dill seed, and crushed red pepper. Bring to a boil.
5. Add drained cucumbers and return to boil. Cover and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Remove garlic cloves.
7. Spoon into hot sterilized jars. Wipe jar rims. Seal.
MY NOTE: This recipe did not call for processing in a hot water bath, so I made half the batch and kept them in the fridge. A similar recipe in The Bernardin Guide to Home Preserving calls for processing 10 minutes at altitudes up to 1000 ft.
